summary: DHH announces Writeboard, 37signals' next product after Basecamp, a focused writing and collaboration tool. He highlights that the first iteration took only five hours and produced just 150 lines of Rails model and controller code. The post serves as both a product announcement and a demonstration of Rails' productivity, emphasizing their 'Less Software' philosophy. DHH expresses excitement about building on a fresh codebase with Rails 0.8 and anticipates a quick release.
key_points: Writeboard is 37signals' follow-up to Basecamp with a narrower focus but potentially broader appeal
The first functional iteration required only 5 hours and 150 lines of Rails code, plus a full test suite
The project exemplifies 37signals' 'Less Software' philosophy of deliberate constraint
Rails 0.8 enables rapid prototyping from scratch, reinforcing DHH's confidence in the framework
money_quotes: It has a more narrow focus than Basecamp, but potentially an even bigger reach.
We're adhering closely to our Less Software approach on this project.
The first iteration, which provided a first stab at much of the core functionality, has taken just around five hours and resulted in 150 lines of model and controller code in Rails.
On top of that, we have a very nice unit and functional test suite exercising all the features.
It's exciting to work on a fresh codebase using Rails 0.8 from scratch and reaffirming as a reminder of just how fast you can get started.
key_insight: A focused product built with disciplined constraints and the right framework can go from zero to functional prototype in hours, not months.
